_readme: The weekly cloud-harvest routine config (propose-only PR flow), ready to POST via the /schedule skill's RemoteTrigger create action.
Blocked 2026-07-16 on GitHub auth: connect GitHub at https://claude.ai/code/onboarding?magic=github-app-setup (install the app on heart-IT/react-brain), then any session can create this verbatim.
Schedule: 30 3 * * 4 = Thursday 09:00 Asia/Calcutta — TWiR ships Tue, React Status Wed, so Thursday catches both fresh.
Regenerate events[0].data.uuid (any lowercase v4) before POSTing.
name: react-brain weekly harvest

message.content:
You are the weekly react-brain harvest agent. This repo is a verified React/React-Native ecosystem knowledge corpus. Your job is one PROPOSE-ONLY harvest pass ending in a pull request — never push to main, never force-push.

SETUP: run `npm install` (single dep: yaml). Then read, in order: `.claude/skills/harvest/SKILL.md` (the routing doc — follow it), `tools/upkeep-routine.md` step 2 (the canonical method; it wins over anything else including this prompt), and `tools/harvest-state.json` (per-newsletter resume state + access notes).

THE PASS:
1. `git checkout -b harvest/<YYYY-MM-DD>`.
2. FIRSTHAND: `node tools/cli.mjs harvest firsthand` — the corpus-derived watch (npm dist-tags/deprecations, GitHub releases, author feeds) diffed vs the committed state. Triage every event per the routine; write `tools/harvest-log/firsthand-<date>.md` (`--manifest` writes a skeleton) with a disposition per event; commit the updated `tools/.firsthand-state.json`. ⚡ TRIPWIRE events are MANDATORY work items — an entry's standing caveat whose release condition came true: do its `then:` action, update the prose caveat, REMOVE the fired tripwire row. When you land a new 'watch/revisit when X ships' caveat, wire it as a `tripwires:` row at keep-time.
3. NEWSLETTERS: for each source in harvest-state.json, check its archive for issues NEWER than last_processed (respect the per-source access notes — some need RSS or specific URL patterns). For each new issue: `node tools/cli.mjs harvest inventory <issue-url>` → triage from that mechanical link list (never from a page summary) → write `tools/harvest-log/<source>-<issue>.md` (line 2: `issue: <issue-url>` — CI re-runs coverage from it) where EVERY external link gets a disposition row carrying its URL (kept → where / already-held → where / skipped → reason class + reopen signal) → `node tools/cli.mjs harvest coverage <issue-url> <manifest>` must exit 0 → apply the verified delta to `skills/react-brain-mentor/entries/*.yaml` → update harvest-state.json (last_processed + count).
4. VERIFICATION IS NON-NEGOTIABLE: fetch-verify every fact and URL you keep (WebFetch → browser-UA curl → Wayback snapshot via archive.org/wayback/available; exclude only if ALL THREE fail). Version/deprecation facts come from registry.npmjs.org JSON, not articles. Never write a URL you did not fetch. Tag reading claims at keep-time (claim ≤260 chars; gates only from the entry's own detect rows). A NEW entry needs the full wiring listed in the skill (entry file + TOC slot + capability_map + FEATURE_DOMAINS + calibrate --seed + count bumps). A formal deprecation ⇒ add the entry's migrate: rule; a supersession-in-progress without formal deprecation ⇒ watch note only, no rule.
5. `node tools/cli.mjs harvest watchlist` — re-triage anything recurring; spot-check all `cap` skips from the previous manifests.
6. `npm test` MUST pass (lint + eval), and `node tools/cli.mjs harvest verify-diff --base=origin/main` MUST exit 0 — it machine-re-verifies every URL you added (direct fetch → Wayback), and CI runs the same gate on your PR, so a receipt you did not actually verify will be caught. If a gate fails because of your edits, fix them; if you cannot fix a specific edit, drop that edit rather than shipping red.
7. Open ONE pull request to main with `gh pr create`: title `harvest: <date> — <headline>`, body = the full narrative ledger note (per-source issues processed, every kept fact with its verification receipt, notable skips with reasons, counts before/after, watchlist findings). The PR body IS the ledger for cloud runs — be thorough there; you have no access to the maintainer's local memory files, so do not attempt the local-ledger steps in the skill.

CALIBRATION: newsletters heavily corroborate each other — the typical honest yield is one gap-filling entry OR a few verified status-flips plus 2-3 readings. A small delta is a sign of health; NEVER pad the delta to look productive. If there are no new issues AND no firsthand events, do not open a PR — just print a summary and stop.

SCOPE: touch only the corpus (skills/react-brain-mentor/**, encyclopedia/), tools/harvest-log/, tools/harvest-state.json, tools/.firsthand-state.json, tools/predictions.jsonl (via calibrate --seed), and count-drift fixes in README/SKILL.md. Never modify site/, bench/, alias/, or tool source code (tools/*.mjs).
